item.title: My Art - My Cosplay
item.description:
My Artcrawl Submission is my cosplay! A final example of my ary is above with me as Ada Wong from Resident Evil -- but to get this to that point is usually super extensive.
First up: I've had to really learn how a camera really works, because all of my work is shot by me - which is why you'll often see me holding a small remote whenever I'm not holding a camera. Shooting your own cosplay not only means learning the ins and outs of a camera over the years, but it also means:
Lighting! This is one of my many "Set" setups. I change them up often, but this is one of my go to backgrounds for initial cosplay tests, and you may notice that behind that curtain is the mirror you often see me take pics in front of for my Imgur posts! I find the mirror post to be the best image to upload here because it shows the full cosplay - but that is always like one picture out of the thousand I take.
I usually have about 4 cosplays in production at any one time. And all of them are either hand crafted by me, or purchased and augmented to fit better and improve the quality. I augment a lot, because if you know me, you might be aware than typically "femme" clothing doesn't fit exactly the way it should on me. I'll leave it at that. Anyway, initial creation is is a ton of work, but is also one of my favorite parts - gathering and augmenting to get it as close as possible to your character is a ton of fun! So, for example - last year I simultaneously had these 4 cosplays all occurring at once: Above is 2B...
I also had Ada Wong in the works...
Elizabeth from Bioshock...
And Classic Scarlet Witch...
While also crafting my Ciri wig!
All of this leads up to the shoot day, and a final product (Me as Mara Jade Above) A shoot day usually involves about 6 hours of prep work before I can even start taking pictures. A full day of shooting usually means I am up from 6am until about 3am (so nearly 24 hours). So by the end of the day I have gotten all the pictures taken and transferred (hours and hours of color correction happens later), and then I am finally able to eat proper food... Well, thanks for checking out my ArtCrawl! Imgur has always been super supportive of me and I love you guys!
item.dc:creator: Alsoashley

item.title: Wedding cake I made
item.description:
Bottom tier for our jigsaw puzzle loving daughter in law
Middle tier for our gamer son
Top tier with bride and groom dinosaurs because the party was in the National History Museum in London and the space was shared with a stegosaurus
item.dc:creator: gingersrule

item.title: I need to know if I have grounds.
item.description: I was showing my children The Lord of the Rings movies (extended, of course) and my wife said that they "drag" and they're "boring". I don't think I have any other choice other than to leave her.

item.title: Willing to receive a different heart but not a vaccine
item.description: Edit - article: https://theguardian.com/us-news/2022/jan/25/heart-transplant-patient-refusing-covid-19-vaccine “Other medical experts have declared their support of the hospital’s policy, noting that the immune system is extremely weak after a transplant, making vaccinations all the more important. “Post any transplant, kidney, heart whatever, your immune system is shut off. The flu could kill you, a cold could kill you, Covid could kill you,” said Dr Arthur Caplan, the head of medical ethics at NYU Grossman School of Medicine, told CBS Boston.”
item.dc:creator: zoraniko

item.title: Mars and it’s child slave cases being buried by the green M n M
item.description: Source: Pocket report
item.dc:creator: Idontevenlikethisapp

item.title: This counts as art, right? My R2-D2.
item.description:
Took me four years to build it. Full size model.
I fabricated a lot of the parts myself with pretty rudimentary tools.
The feet are all steel. I did not weld these, got them secondhand from another builder.
I had to learn a lot. I knew nothing about servos or RC stuff.
I designed the legs attachments to come apart so I can transport him in pieces. He weighs about 150 pounds.
Before and after weathering. A clean droid just doesn’t look right to me.
When the weather is nice my ten year old son drives him around on the sidewalk and all the neighborhood kids run outside. The plan was to do hospital visits but then the pandemic hit so that’s on hold.
item.dc:creator: LeastViral

item.title: Day 242: of cheeseburgers I want inside of me

Map of all burger spots that are available on yelp! Big thanks to @OhBoyDoIHaveNews4U for this. Link to his post and map https://imgur.com/gallery/vcObDuD

item.dc:creator: HibiscusQueen79

item.title: Wood and acrylic portraits… I call my style “Wallffiti”
item.description:
Friend of mine who is a visual artist based in Durham, NC: Candy Carver sent me a photo…
I take the photo into Photoshop and illustrator to add my own style and color space to plan out the painting later…
The wood is hand sculpted. That’s me.
Table saw, band saw, sander to create my wood template for the design…
Design is then printed onto the sculpted wood. Now it’s time to paint…
Each individual piece is now hand painted with the aid of an assistant. I work with apprentices via a paid art therapy arrangement.
Almost done.
Finished.
Staged at a gallery in Raleigh-The Museum…
That’s ALSO a barbershop.
item.dc:creator: johnsonjoeb
